title: backports for v4.0.0
body:
add support in 4.0 for healthcheck on failure actions

Signed-off-by: Charlie Doern <cdoern@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign jwhonce for approval by writing `/assign @jwhonce` in a comment. For more information see:[The Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/v4.0.0-rhel/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL, I think I did this right :)
@jwhonce for this to work would we not need to backport these changes to 4.0 podman as well so that the json is valid? or am I missing something else as to why gating tests are failing
I was suspecting this would go against the v4.0.0 branch from upstream?  I'm not seeing a v4.0.0-rhel branch (at least at this moment).  @jwhonce PTAL
Code changes LGTM though, once the gating is figured out.  That might be related to the branch.

> I was suspecting this would go against the v4.0.0 branch from upstream? I'm not seeing a v4.0.0-rhel branch (at least at this moment). @jwhonce PTAL

I am seeing a v4.2 branch and a v4.0.0-rhel branch (which I created)

This is where I was expecting to see the v4.2.0-release:  https://github.com/containers/podman-py/releases.  I'm only seeing the v4.2.0, v4.0.0, and a variety of v3.* branches there.  

I'm showing my Git release ignorance here, perhaps the v4.2.0-release branch will be created (or more likely you'll need to create it) after this merges.

Just noting for future reference, that the .cirrus.yml file was removed per @cevich 's recommendation as the majority of the tests were failing in this branch, and they could not be fixed, at least easily.

This addresses: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2132361

Charlie, were you able to test locally to make sure stuff worked as expected?
@cdoern, excellent, thanks for the update.  Are you preparing the v4.0.0-rhel branch and a new release for Jindrich?  Or do we have another delivery mechanism for him for tomorrow?
